speaker: Susan
content: Hey, you. What are we having?

speaker: Mike
content: I talked to Julie and she suggested rib-eye steaks. She said that's your favorite.
mark: talked to sb 和某人交谈、谈话 tell sb sth 告诉某人（某事）
rib-eye steaks 肋眼牛排 steak n.肉排，（尤指）牛排；鱼排 rib n.肋骨；排骨

speaker: Susan
content: I love my steak.

speaker: Mike
content: Eddie, Hey, I'm sorry about your house. How you holding up?
mark: How you holding up? 你还撑得住吗 hold up 支撑，支持，举起 完整的形式应该是 "How are you holding up?" 这里的 "are" 被省略了，但在口语中，这种省略是很常见的，尤其是在非正式的场合。

speaker: Eddie
content: All right, I guess. Oh, is somebody having a party?

speaker: Mike
content: No, Susan's just throwing me one of her traditional welcome-to-the-neighborhood dinners. Only I'm cooking, and having it at my house.
mark: throw sb a dinner 为某人举办一场晚宴；设宴招待某人; 这里的“throw”是一个俚语，意思是“举办”或“准备”，通常用于非正式的场合。
at my house 人们更习惯用at来表达 在家（里做某事）这样的含义。

speaker: Eddie
content: Traditional? I didn't get one.

speaker: Susan
content: Oh, it's sort of a new tradition.

speaker: Mike
content: It won't be anything fancy. Just a little home cooking.
mark: fancy adj. 时髦的，奢华的；华丽的，花哨的；复杂的 v. 喜欢，想要
little 很少；几乎没有，带有否定的意味。
a little 一点儿；少量，表示肯定的意思

speaker: Eddie
content: That sounds so good. I've been having nothing but fast food lately.
mark: nothing but 只有；仅仅；只不过

speaker: Alice
content: Susan suddenly had an awful feeling in the pit of her stomach.
mark: in the pit of her stomach 在她的胃里 pit n.坑，洼；疤痕，凹陷 v. 使竞争，使对立；这个短语通常形容一种深深的不安或恐惧感，就像在胃里有一个坑一样。

speaker: Alice
content: As if she was watching an accident in slow motion. She knew it would happen, but was powerless to stop it.
mark: as if 好像，仿佛
in slow motion 以慢动作的方式

speaker: Susan
content: Eddie, would you like to join us for dinner?

speaker: Eddie
content: Oh, that's so sweet. No, I don't want to intrude, three's a crowd.
mark: intrude  v.闯入；打扰，侵扰；
three's a crowd 三个人太多了

speaker: Mike
content: No, it's not like that. I mean, Susan's bringing Julie.
mark: it's not like that 不是那样的；并非如此

speaker: Susan
content: It's not like that. The more the merrier.
mark: The more the merrier 越多越好，多多益善。通常用来表示欢迎更多的人参与某件事情 merry adj.<旧>愉快的，高兴的；<旧>令人愉快的

speaker: Eddie
content: Well, this will be fun.

speaker: Mike
content: All right, tomorrow night. We'll eat at six.

speaker: Eddie
content: Great. Oh, Susan, this will make up for the dinner that you never threw me.
mark: make up for 弥补，补偿，抵消

speaker: Mike
content: Should I have told her we were having steak? She's not a vegetarian or something similar?
mark: Should I have told sb sth? 我本应该告诉某人某事吗？
vegetarian n. 素食者，素食主义者；食草动物 adj. 素食的，素的
or something similar或者类似的事物；或诸如此类的东西，常用于在列举事物后，补充说明还有其他与之类似的情况或事物。

speaker: Susan
content: Oh, no. Eddie's definitely a carnivore.
mark: definitely adv. 肯定地，当然；明确地，确定地
carnivore n. 食肉动物；喜欢吃肉的人，食肉动物
